ai_research.headline: Google ships Gemma 4 under Apache 2.0, Gemini 3.1 gets Flash-Lite and Flash Live modes, Claude Sonnet 4.6 remains king for coding

items.title: Google releases Gemma 4 — most capable open models, now Apache 2.0
items.body: Google dropped Gemma 4, billed as the most capable open models byte-for-byte, now fully under Apache 2.0 license. This is a major move for open-source AI — permissive licensing removes barriers for commercial use. Part of Google's dual strategy: proprietary Gemini for consumers, open Gemma for the ecosystem.

items.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite and Flash Live launched
items.body: Google shipped Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite (their fastest, most budget-friendly model) and Gemini 3.1 Flash Live (real-time audio AI). Enhanced multimodal capabilities for voice and visual processing. Flash-Lite is aimed at high-throughput, low-latency workloads — think real-time agent inference at scale.

items.title: Claude Sonnet 4.6 still the go-to — Claude 5 Opus expected Q2-Q3
items.body: No new Claude release in April. Sonnet 4.6 (1M context, faster than 4.5) remains Anthropic's best public model. Claude 5 Opus rumored for Q2-Q3 2026. Claude Mythos 5 (10T params, cybersecurity) confirmed to exist but won't ship publicly. Anthropic previewed a new smaller/faster Claude variant on April 7 for SMBs.

items.title: GPT-5.4 holds the crown — no new OpenAI releases this month
items.body: GPT-5.4 (March 5) remains OpenAI's latest. No new drops in April. GPT-5.1 was retired March 11. GPT-5.4 mini is the workhorse for cost-sensitive deployments. OpenAI focused on enterprise integrations rather than new model launches.

items.title: Grok 5 rumors intensify — 6T params, MoE, Colossus 2 training
items.body: Speculation mounting for Grok 5 public beta in April-May 2026. Rumored 6 trillion parameters, Mixture-of-Efforts architecture, trained on Colossus 2 supercomputer. Musk has hinted at 'AGI-level' capability. Grok 4.20 and its Multi-agent variant remain the current production models.

ai_research.learn_today: Gemma 4 under Apache 2.0 is the big open-source play. If you're building agents on a budget, this is the model to evaluate — Google is serious about ecosystem lock-in through permissive licensing.

openclaw_agents.headline: Anthropic launches Managed Agents public beta, Claude Mythos finds zero-days, LangGraph+MCP remains production standard

items.title: Anthropic Managed Agents enters public beta
items.body: Anthropic shipped Managed Agents to public beta — faster deployment of AI agents for complex, multi-step tasks. This is Anthropic's answer to the agentic frameworks race. Native integration with Claude models means lower latency and better tool-use reliability than third-party orchestration.
items.try_this: If you're using Claude for agent workloads, Managed Agents is worth testing for complex task chains.

items.title: Claude Mythos uncovers thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities
items.body: Anthropic's preview of Claude Mythos showed it discovering thousands of zero-day security vulnerabilities. Major validation for agentic AI in cybersecurity. The model won't ship publicly, but the results signal where frontier agentic capability is heading.

items.title: LangGraph + MCP remains the dominant production agent stack
items.body: LangGraph (cyclic, stateful agent graphs) combined with MCP (Model Context Protocol, the 'USB-C for AI') is the top recommendation for enterprise agent deployment in 2026. MCP now Linux Foundation governed with 50+ enterprise partners. Semantic caching in LangGraph-MCP reduces latency 35-50%.
items.try_this: Review the DZone LangGraph-MCP integration guide for production architecture patterns.

items.title: NVIDIA AITune — auto-optimizing inference for agents
items.body: NVIDIA released AITune, an open-source toolkit that automatically selects the fastest inference backend for PyTorch models. Critical for scaling agent infrastructure — takes the guesswork out of model serving optimization.

items.title: Perplexity pivots to AI agents
items.body: Search engine Perplexity is shifting focus from search to agentic capabilities. Signals the broader industry trend: search is commoditized, agents are the next battleground. Every AI company is becoming an agent company.

openclaw_agents.try_today: Anthropic Managed Agents in public beta is the newest option for Claude-native agentic workflows. Worth a quick eval if you're building multi-step agent pipelines.

tesla.note: TSLA at $352.42, UBS upgraded to Neutral from Sell with $352 PT. Stock hit 2026 low last week, on track for 8-week losing streak. Q1 deliveries disappointed at 358K. Q1 earnings April 22 — consensus EPS -$0.24. JPMorgan still bearish, sees 60% downside. But: Tesla reviving cheaper EV plans including a new SUV, Giga Texas producing Cybercabs. Terafab chip project with Intel announced. After-hours momentum to $356.

tesla_spacex_xai.spacex: SpaceX IPO is THE story. Confidentially filed April 1, targeting $1.75T valuation post-xAI merger — potentially the largest IPO ever. Analyst day April 21. Multiple Starlink Falcon 9 launches continue (Group 377, 376, 17-21, 10-24). Starship Flight 12 delayed to early May. SpaceX targeting five uncrewed Mars missions by end-2026. IPO buzz lifting broader aerospace stocks.
tesla_spacex_xai.xai: Grok Imagine got Speed and Quality modes (April 3), with Pro mode (1080p) coming late April at $30/mo SuperGrok tier. X rolled out automatic post translation powered by Grok and AI photo editor on iOS (April 8). Swiss Finance Minister filed criminal complaint over Grok 'roast' post (April 1). xAI acquired by SpaceX in Feb 2026. Grok 5 rumors building for April-May release. Terafab chip project announced — Tesla-SpaceX-Intel joint chip manufacturing in Austin for EVs, robots, and space data centers.

items.title: BEEF: Season 2
items.note: Emmy-winning anthology returns with a new story — a blackmail feud with dark comedy twists. Drops April 16. The first season was a masterpiece, high expectations here.

items.title: Crooks: Season 2
items.note: German crime drama heist sequel with escalating rivalries. Drops today (April 14). Solid first season, worth continuing if you liked it.

items.title: Untold: Jail Blazers
items.note: Sports documentary series about the Portland Trail Blazers chaos. Part of Netflix's excellent Untold series. Drops today (April 14).

items.title: Ronaldinho: The One and Only
items.note: Brazilian docuseries on the soccer legend. Drops April 16. Could be great for a sports doc weekend.

items.title: The Law According to Lidia Poët: Season 3
items.note: Italian period drama returns. Charming but niche — wait for reviews if you're not already invested.

items.title: Funny AF with Kevin Hart
items.note: Comedy competition series. Drops April 20. Hart is reliable but competition shows are hit or miss. Wait for buzz.

movies.upcoming: Stranger Things: Tales From '85 (April 23), Apex movie (April 24). Mark calendars.

items.title: Jasper AI shifts to agentic workflows — execution over drafting
items.body: Jasper moved from content drafting to full campaign execution with AI agents. Governance features for brand consistency at scale. Their 2026 State of AI Marketing report (1,400 marketers) confirms AI is now core to marketing ops. Scale and governance are the top barriers.

items.title: HubSpot Breeze AI embeds into all hubs — predictive agents for CRM
items.body: HubSpot's Breeze suite now powers AI agents across Marketing, Sales, and Service hubs. Lead scoring, email personalization, content optimization, and AI search engine optimization all automated. Free tier available, pro from $20/mo.

items.title: Agentic marketing tools flooding the market
items.body: The martech stack is going fully agentic. Jasper for campaign execution, HubSpot for CRM automation, Surfer SEO for content optimization, and newcomers like Gumloop for no-code AI automations. Human-in-the-loop is becoming human-ON-the-loop. The tools execute; humans review.

macro.mood: Monday morning, markets flat. BTC surging at $74.4K (+5%), strongest crypto move in weeks. TSLA flat at $352 after UBS upgrade to Neutral. SpaceX IPO analyst day April 21 is the week's big event. Q1 earnings season kicking off — Tesla reports April 22.
